Taxonomy_y: Eukaryota; Metazoa; Ecdysozoa; Arthropoda; Hexapoda; Insecta; Pterygota; Neoptera; Endopterygota; Lepidoptera; Glossata; Ditrysia; Pyraloidea; Crambidae; Pyraustinae; Loxostege
Assembly: The assembly ilLoxStic1.hap1.1 is based on 51x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by Project Psyche (https://www.projectpsyche.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. The Z chromosome was identified by coverage and Merian elements, the W chromosome by coverage. Chromosome 1 might be a autosomal fusion of an ancestral W and potentially sex linked.

Taxonomy_y: Eukaryota; Metazoa; Chordata; Craniata; Vertebrata; Euteleostomi; Archelosauria; Archosauria; Dinosauria; Saurischia; Theropoda; Coelurosauria; Aves; Neognathae; Neoaves; Telluraves; Australaves; Passeriformes; Sittidae; Sitta
Assembly: This project provides the diploid genome assembly bSitEur1.1 of the Eurasian nuthatch, Sitta europaea. The sample was provided by Jan T. Lifjeld, and the data generation and assembly were carried out by EBP-Nor (https://www.ebpnor.org/). bSitEur is based on 34x PacBio HiFi data and 88x Arima Hi-C data. The HiFi reads were filtered for adapters using HiFiAdapterFilt, and assembled with hifiasm along with Hi-C data to generate a pair of haplotype-resolved assemblies. K-mers from the two assemblies were used to filter Hi-C reads to create two sets of reads for scaffolding the assemblies (i.e., reads used to scaffold hap1 did not contain k-mers unique to hap2). YaHS was used for scaffolding. FCS-adaptor was used to remove putative adapters, and putative contaminants were removed with FCS-GX. The chromosomes confirmed by Hi-C data are named according to size.

Assembly: The assembly of Acrocephalus paludicola (bAcrPal2.1) is based on 129X ONT data and Dovetail Omni-C Hi-C data generated as part of the ERGA-pilot programme (ERGA, https://www.erga-biodiversity.eu/). The assembly process included the following steps: initial ONT assembly generation with NextDeNovo, polishing of the consensus using Medaka (with long-reads) and Hapo-G (with short-reads), removal of contaminant sequences using Decap, removal of haplotypic duplications using purge_dups, and Hi-C-based scaffolding with 3D-DNA. In total, no contigs were identified as contaminants (bacterial, archaeal, or viral). Additionally, 306 regions totaling 162 Mb were identified as haplotypic duplications and removed. Finally, the primary assembly was analyzed and manually improved using Pretext.

Assembly: Primary assembly. Maternal haplotig of Trio-binned genome of a male wild-type naked mole-rat. Reads in the initial assebmly were a combination of the maternal- and unassigned- reads. Assembly was completed by a combination of the vertebrate genome project and DNA-zoo projects. Mitochondrial genomes were assembled with the mitoVGP pipeline. Y-chromosome contigs were generated with a custom pipeline that levaraged male and female WGS to find strongly male-biased contigs. Male-biased contigs were re-assembled with public male NMR Hi-C data. This assembly was then upraded by employing in silico chromosome painting of the draft scaffolds in the assembly against the American Porcupine (EreDor1) assembly and against the NMR FISH-Karyoptype published in Romanenko et al., 2023 (PMID: 380307020). This upgrade corrected intrachromosomal misassemblies, placed small contigs, and placed centromeres. Ambiguous syntenic alignments were resolved by inspecting our Hi-C-seq data at the end points of those regions. Chromosome names were identified in collaboration with GeneCodehc.

Assembly: The assembly ilAnaPras2.1 is based on 135x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. A manually phased assembly (based on HiC signal) was generated from a jointly curated primary and alt assembly. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Chromosome Z was assigned based on synteny to GCA_963853795.1.

Assembly: Paternal haplotig of Trio-binned genome of a male wild-type naked mole-rat. Reads in the initial assebmly were a combination of the paternal- and unassigned- reads. Assembly was completed by a combination of the vertebrate genome project and DNA-zoo projects. Mitochondrial genomes were assembled with the mitoVGP pipeline. Y-chromosome contigs were generated with a custom pipeline that levaraged male and female WGS to find strongly male-biased contigs. Male-biased contigs were re-assembled with public male NMR Hi-C data.

Assembly: Primary assembly. Maternal haplotig of Trio-binned genome of a male wild-type naked mole-rat. Reads in the initial assebmly were a combination of the maternal- and unassigned- reads. Assembly was completed using trio-binning, the Flye aligner, and polishing, 10X-scaffolding, and Hi-C scaffolding piplines established in both the vertebrate genome project and DNA-zoo projects. Mitochondrial genomes were assembled with the mitoVGP pipeline. Y-chromosome contigs were generated with a custom pipeline that levaraged male and female WGS to find strongly male-biased contigs. Male-biased contigs were re-assembled with public male NMR Hi-C data.
